findings.claim_text: By late May 2026 the shares had at one point risen nearly 1,600% since listing, lifting the market value above HK$880B (about US$112B).

In H1 2025, about 84.8% of revenue came from on-premise/private deployment — high-touch, project-based systems work for Chinese government and state-owned buyers — while the cloud API that is MaaS contributed only 15.2%.
contrary to some reporting, the Zhipu entries carry no "Footnote 4" or military-end-user designation — they reference only Section 744.11 of the EAR.
